coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 45–50 min Z2, no vert work. Your resting HR is 8 bpm above your 30-day average (70 vs 62.2) and recovery score sits at 57/100—you're not fully settled. You logged 17.2 miles this week already and you're in taper; the job now is to flush metabolic waste and maintain aerobic feel, not accumulate stress. Keep HR between 120–130 bpm and run a route without hills. This is a maintenance day, not a training day.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You slept 9.9 hours last night—excellent—but your resting HR suggests you may still be metabolically fatigued from the load you've carried into taper. Prioritize 1.2–1.5g carbs per kg bodyweight today spread across 4–5 meals, with focus on simple carbs post-run (40–50g within 30 min). If you're traveling for work today, pack rice cakes, dates, or a recovery drink; don't rely on airline/hotel food to hit targets. Sodium intake should be normal—no aggressive loading yet; that comes in the final two weeks.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Last night's 9.9 hours is a positive signal, but elevated resting HR + moderate recovery score suggest your nervous system is still clearing load. Aim for 8–9 hours tonight as well. During the day: 10–15 min of easy mobility work (hips, calves, T-spine), no foam rolling aggression. If you're in-office at MNTN, take two 5-min walking breaks to keep movement gentle. Cold baths or ice are not necessary today; focus on sleep quality and consistency instead.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 60 days out with 4+ weeks of taper ahead—this is where doubt creeps in because volume drops and race pace feels foreign. Recognize that: your aerobic base is solid (consistent 120–140 bpm efforts logged), the Alps' 2,500m vert is manageable in your fitness window, and the next 4 weeks are about sharpness and specificity, not panic. Stay disciplined with the plan. Trust the numbers, not the nervous system.
